It will try its level best to enhance productivity by bringing creativity & innovation by introducing small and medium size enterprises.
Business Incubation Center (BIC)

title: Entrepreneurship Development Center (EDC) – IMSciences
text:
el best to enhance productivity by bringing creativity & innovation by introducing small and medium size enterprises.
Business Incubation Center (BIC)
The Business Incubation Center (BIC), IMSciences was established in 2018, with the assistance of Higher Education Commission (HEC), Pakistan in order to promote entrepreneurship in the region. For this cause, IMSciences has developed necessary infrastructure where aspiring entrepreneurs are groomed to advance their business ventures. The rudimentary purpose of BIC is to promote business ventures that are not only self-subsistent but also carry financial prospect. The incubated startups are exposed to a number of services and resources beneficial for their business development. Any successful business incubate would prove to be an asset for the society. It would help create new technologies, more jobs, and develop a much needed entrepreneurial ecosystem in the region.

title: Entrepreneurship Development Center (EDC) – IMSciences
text:
e to be an asset for the society. It would help create new technologies, more jobs, and develop a much needed entrepreneurial ecosystem in the region.
The active program provides customized consultancy services, trainings, well-educated workforce, financial and marketing assistance as well as technological expertise to the business incubates. Resultantly, a number of different services would be provided for the growth of new business ventures, all under one roof.
Objectives
Promoting Self-employment/ Entrepreneurship among youth.
Improving efficiency of startup companies and young entrepreneurs.
Helping business development in fields of Finance, Marketing and Management.
Assisting startups in network development.

The Centre for Excellence in Islamic Finance (CEIF) is collaboratively established by the Institute of Management Sciences, Peshawar and the State Bank of Pakistan to spur financial inclusion in Pakistan.
The strategy of the CEIF is designed on threefold orientations namely academia, research, and industry efforts to boost-up Islamic finance and financial inclusion through it. The CEIF provides a long-term and sustained platform through which the educationalists, researchers, and practitioners alike take benefit to holistically advance the Islamic Finance and hence the financial inclusion of the society.

title: Center for Excellence in Islamic Finance (CEIF) – IMSciences
text:
lists, researchers, and practitioners alike take benefit to holistically advance the Islamic Finance and hence the financial inclusion of the society.
The CEIF is established through a highly competitive process started in January 2015. The Federal Finance Minister Pakistan, the Governor State Bank of Pakistan, and the Chairman Higher Education Commission have jointly invited the VCs of Universities in Pakistan to submit proposals for the establishment of the centres. After multiple stages, competitive evaluations and presentations IMSciences is awarded the financial innovation challenge fund to establish the CEIF, alongside LUMS and IBA, the two other leading business schools in the country. Since, then CEIF IMSciences became the country’s largest Islamic finance education provider, offering PhD, MBA, and PGD in Islamic finance. CEIF has also produced cutting-edge research and industry impact in collaboration with the world leading resource centres such as Durham Centre for Islamic Finance, IRTI, Islamic Development Bank, Brunel University UK, and All Islamic Banks in the Country”.
Vision
To be the premier centre for Islamic finance research, education, and practice.
Mission

IMSciences has developed centers of excellence that are dedicated to developing academic fields and functional responsibilities in high growth areas with little current academic provision. To meet these needs, the centers are engaged in providing vital training services, certifications, and research input to government sector, bilateral, and multilateral agencies.
Human Resource Development Center (HRDC)

title: Centers – IMSciences
text:
ning services, certifications, and research input to government sector, bilateral, and multilateral agencies.
Human Resource Development Center (HRDC)
The HRDC is one of the premier and most accomplished centers of the Institute. It was established in 2003 in line with the Institute’s strategic imperative to enhance and strengthen its overall scope not only in terms of academic programs but also through subject/discipline specific centers. Besides conducting applied and need-based research to generate knowledge, the center also assists the Institute to improve and enrich its own organizational structure as well as that of other public and private sector organizations by providing intensive human resource training and capacity building program.
HRDC operates on the entrepreneurial philosophy of self-reliance and self-sustenance: all projects and day to-day operations are dependent on the resources generated by the HRDC network. In this way, the HRDC makes tangible contributions to its parent organization besides adding to the overall value.
In 2009-10, the center conducted Capacity Building, Research & Development, and Technical Assistance projects with:

Center for Public Policy Research (CPPR)
The Center for Public Policy Research at the Institute of Management Sciences (IMSciences) serves as an independent Peshawar-based think tank on public policy and related issues, producing key pieces of policy-oriented research and providing capacity building of public sector professionals in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. Based on its impartial and nonpartisan approach, the CPPR has been arranging seminars, symposiums, debates, panel discussions, workshops, lectures and talks on various aspects of social, political and economic policies in Pakistan, in general, and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa /FATA, in particular.

Centre of Excellence (CoE) for High-Tech & Digital Skills
The Centre of Excellence (CoE) for High Tech and Digital Skills in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa is a state-of-the-art training facility established at the Institute of Management Sciences (IMSciences), Peshawar, co-funded by the European Union, and implemented by the British Council. The CoE is designed to provide women across KP with access to quality technical and vocational education, empowering them with globally recognized skills in emerging digital and high-tech fields.

The Center for Water Informatics and Climate Resilience (CWC) provides national leadership by collaborating with federal and provincial governments, private sector stakeholders, and academia to address water and climate change policies and strategies. It works to build institutional capacity in a sustainable and systematic manner.
Goals of CWC:
Establish a national forum for discourses on climate resilience and rational use of water.
Develop and present policy alternatives to support federal and provincial governments in formulating comprehensive water and climate change policies.
Facilitate collaboration between development, corporate, and IT sectors with climate and water experts to address key challenges and solutions.
Advocate for climate resilience and sustainable water use at all levels of government, civil society, and the corporate sector.

title: Center for Water Informatics and Climate Resilience (CWC) – IMSciences
text:
llenges and solutions.
Advocate for climate resilience and sustainable water use at all levels of government, civil society, and the corporate sector.
Foster national and international networking on water governance and climate resilience, particularly with universities.
Promote research excellence by increasing the number of grants for water governance and climate resilience projects at the provincial, national, and international levels.
Provide consultations and resources to government and industry.
Deliver education and training programs to enhance the skillset needed by government and private sectors through diploma, undergraduate, and graduate programs.
The Center is led by a Coordinator, supported by a diverse faculty of fellows specializing in fields such as climate change, disaster risk reduction (DRR), water governance, public policy, gender, econometrics, and project management. External experts are engaged as needed.

title: Center for Water Informatics and Climate Resilience (CWC) – IMSciences
text:
disaster risk reduction (DRR), water governance, public policy, gender, econometrics, and project management. External experts are engaged as needed.
CWC also facilitates policy development, provides analysis, conducts research, and acts as a resource hub for climate change and water governance. It aspires to become a leading organization addressing these challenges in Pakistan, particularly in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, including merged districts.
Aim
To establish a world-class think tank rooted in local knowledge and research in one of the countries most affected by climate change.
Mission
To generate and share knowledge on water governance and climate change, with a focus on adaptation and finance, aiding communities in the Global South to build resilience against climate change.
Through partnerships with organizations like GIZ, The World Bank, UN agencies, and others, CWC fosters grounded, relevant knowledge for the benefit of developing countries, donors, and NGOs.
Key Achievements
In a short period, CWC has collaborated with partners including the Dutch and Swiss governments, HEC, USAID, DFID, WWF, and various federal and provincial departments. Notable projects include:

title: Center for Water Informatics and Climate Resilience (CWC) – IMSciences
text:
h partners including the Dutch and Swiss governments, HEC, USAID, DFID, WWF, and various federal and provincial departments. Notable projects include:
Development of environmental and social management frameworks for WWF’s Recharge Pakistan Project (funded by GCF).
Climate risk analysis for District Battagram, KP (funded by KNH).
Climate advocacy and coordination for resilient action (funded by the German Red Cross).
Achieving SDG 6 through inclusive urban water governance (funded by The World Bank and HEC).
Enhancing transboundary water cooperation in the Kabul River Basin (funded by PSDP).
Development of the Provincial Disaster Management Plan (2022-2027) (funded by PDMA-KP).
Youth-focused climate and social cohesion initiatives in KP (funded by Islamic Relief).
Local climate adaptation plans for districts in Sindh and Balochistan (funded by IRC).
Technical and Financial Capacity
CWC boasts technical expertise, a skilled team, and strong ties with government institutions, communities, and industries. CWC-IMSciences ensures robust financial management, including bid security and bank guarantees.
Quality Assurance

title: Center for Water Informatics and Climate Resilience (CWC) – IMSciences
text:
utions, communities, and industries. CWC-IMSciences ensures robust financial management, including bid security and bank guarantees.
Quality Assurance
CWC maintains high standards in program delivery through internal and external monitoring systems, overseen by the Team Lead, who ensures smooth logistics and implementation..

title: Facilities – IMSciences
text:
ON-CAMPUS SERVICES
Our newly built technology based campus with additional academic as well as recreational facilities has further added to the learning experience of the students. Our seventy canal campus has four blocks with a broad range of dedicated facilities including state-of-the-art classrooms, libraries, computer labs, theatre rooms, conference rooms, recreational spots, and dormitories.
Student Counseling Service
Â The Student Counseling Service is a facility through which students can receive counseling regarding study skills, career decisions, substance abuse, and personal problems such as anxiety or depression. The SCS also provides a wide range of services to faculty and staff with the help of their team of professional counselors and clinical psychologists. The SCS provides individual & group counseling, and workshops to enhance students’ academic performance in the most congenial environment. Individual counseling assists students in resolving issues that might cause emotional distress and interfere with personal and academic objectives.

title: Facilities – IMSciences
text:
t. Individual counseling assists students in resolving issues that might cause emotional distress and interfere with personal and academic objectives.
Along with âCareer Counselingâ, the CSC is aiming to enhance job-related skills of the graduates as well as alumni in terms of their CV formation, interview preparation as well as search for employment. The Centre is facilitating different sector employers towards their recruitment process by providing them quality human capital of the Institute. The establishment of CSC is basically demand driven as previously, the career services were partially rendered by the Institute Coordinators who could not afford ample time investment due to their involvement in primary job responsibilities. Hence they forwarded the demand to the management to establish a separate placement office and staff dedicated to student career services. The graduates of IMSciences have expressed their gratitude at receiving a formal platform that caters to the career services in a professional manner. With the efforts of the center and the intellectual merit of the students, the roster of employers is quite diverse and includes the given institutions:
CitiBank

OTHER ON-CAMPUS FACILITIES
COMPUTER LABS
Currently, the institute has five computer laboratories with dual core systems and high speed internet: most systems are Microsoft Windows XP based with some multi booted consisting of Linux. All computers within the campus are interconnected to a server controlled by the network administrator. For students of IT all the professional software is provided at the Labs: indeed, there is a separate DLD and Electronics Lab dedicated to students in the Computer Science Program.
LIBRARIES

title: Facilities – IMSciences
text:
software is provided at the Labs: indeed, there is a separate DLD and Electronics Lab dedicated to students in the Computer Science Program.
LIBRARIES
The Institute libraries contain books related to a range of academic and nonacademic fields. The library has separate male and female reading rooms. Library cards are also issued to students in order to facilitate book loans for a certain period of time.
DORMITORIES
IMSciences has two innovative dorm buildings for male and female students respectively: the dorms are facilitated with all the basic necessities, high speed internet as well as phone lines besides providing a conducive academic environment that is a residential extension of the intellectual culture within IMSciences. All the dorms are supervised by wardens who are responsible for residential, medical, and social welfare of the students.
TRANSPORT

title: Facilities – IMSciences
text:
within IMSciences. All the dorms are supervised by wardens who are responsible for residential, medical, and social welfare of the students.
TRANSPORT
For students living in the cultural city of Peshawar, the Institute provides sophisticated transportation at reasonable charges. The Institute has its own transportation network supervised by a coordinator in order to meet the requirements of students, faculty, and the staff. Presently, the Institute has a fleet of four large and a single mini bus. Moreover, cabs and public transport buses are available almost round the clock at stops adjacent to the campus. Neighboring the southern boundary wall of the campus is a bus stand and a small market ready to become operational.
CLASSROOMS
Unlike most business and management institutes, at IMSciences all classrooms are equipped with comfortable seating, ventilation system, split air conditioners, digital rostrums, and multimedia projectors. Classrooms are designed to accommodate 40 students per room; however, student groups are voluntarily kept smaller in order to facilitate reasonable student-instructor interaction.
THEATRE ROOMS

title: Facilities – IMSciences
text:
tudents per room; however, student groups are voluntarily kept smaller in order to facilitate reasonable student-instructor interaction.
THEATRE ROOMS
Each Academic block has an arch-shaped Theatre Room equipped with central cooling system, convertible furniture, digital boards, digital rostrums and multimedia projectors. Theatres are used for various academic purposes including annual lectures, seminars, and symposia.
HEALTH CENTRE
Health Centre at IMSciences is an appropriately equipped, two bed clinic open to any current student and staff member. Headed by an experienced medical professional and family medicine specialist, it provides services such as:
Medical treatment for injuries and illness
Counseling for stress reduction, mental health crisis, change in behavioral pattern, smoking cessation, nutrition and more.
Advice on hygiene and preventive health.
CAFE
The campus has an IMCafe that serves students from morning till late evening. During Ramazaan Sehri service is also available. Besides catering for food and drinks, some daily need items are also available.

Human Resource Development Center (HRDC)
The HRDC is one of the premier and most accomplished centers of the Institute. It was established in 2003 in line with the Institute’s strategic imperative to enhance and strengthen its overall scope not only in terms of academic programs but also through subject/discipline specific centers. Besides conducting applied and need-based research to generate knowledge, the center also assists the Institute to improve and enrich its own organizational structure as well as that of other public and private sector organizations by providing intensive human resource training and capacity building program.
HRDC operates on the entrepreneurial philosophy of self-reliance and self-sustenance: all projects and day to-day operations are dependent on the resources generated by the HRDC network. In this way, the HRDC makes tangible contributions to its parent organization besides adding to the overall value.

title: IMSciences Admission Test Preparation: Tutorial & Mock Test for Applicants – IMSciences
text:
IMSciences Admission Test Preparation: Tutorial & Mock Test for Applicants
Tutorial for Entry Test at IMSciences
To help applicants prepare for the IMSciences Admission Test, we have introduced a tutorial. The test will be conducted on a computer, where candidates will answer multiple-choice questions directly on the screen.
Mock Test for Admission Applicants at IMSciences
https://ASQscholar.com/mock
To help applicants become familiar with the Computer-Based Admission Test (CBT) environment, IMSciences provides a Mock Test facility before the actual examination.
The mock test enables applicants to:
Experience the computer-based testing interface.
Understand how to navigate between questions.
Practice selecting and changing answers.
Learn how to review and submit the test.
Build confidence and reduce test-day anxiety.
Applicants are strongly encouraged to attempt the mock test prior to the actual admission test to ensure a smooth and comfortable testing experience.

title: Academic Linkages & Exchange Office (ALEO) – IMSciences
text:
Academic Linkages & Exchange Office (ALEO)
Established in 2017 at the Institute of Management Sciences, the Office of Academic Linkages & Exchange Office (ALEO) serves as the institutional hub for national and international academic engagement. The office is dedicated to expanding educational, research, and professional development opportunities through strategic collaborations with leading universities across Pakistan and worldwide.
ALEO’s internationalization approach extends beyond student mobility, emphasizing joint research initiatives, knowledge exchange, collaborative training, and the promotion of multicultural understanding. By fostering sustainable academic partnerships, the office strengthens global connectivity and institutional excellence.
Supported by a dedicated team and robust operational capacity, ALEO manages multiple programs concurrently while providing comprehensive end-to-end support. Services include guidance on official documentation, coordination, language assistance, orientation programs, accommodation support, and, where available, financial aid facilitation, ensuring a seamless and enriching international experience for students and staff alike.

Introduction The Institute of Management Sciences (IMSciences) Library came into being with the inception of IMSciences in 1995. From 1995 to December 31st 2001, the library remained inside the premises of the University of Peshawar. In January 2002, however, IMSciences moved to Hayatabad Peshawar where initially the library comprised of two small rooms. In August 2007, however, the library shifted to phase 7, where again a temporary but wide hall was allotted to the Library. In September 2010 the library finally shifted to its own new building on 5th floor of the Academic Block.
Vision Our vision is to become the intellectual center of the Institute by fully integrating its services, state-of-the-art technologies, and unique collections into all aspects of our institution.
Mission The IMSciences Library aims to:

title: Library – IMSciences
text:
ating its services, state-of-the-art technologies, and unique collections into all aspects of our institution.
Mission The IMSciences Library aims to:
facilitate library users’ access to information resources provide latest information through updated edition of books and periodicals provide excellent readers’ advisory services, provide modern library services including printing, scanning, photocopying and online public access cataloguing (OPAC) serve as a center for learning by providing a place for students and faculty to actively engage in study and research; use technology to extend access to library resources and services; and assist and instruct students and faculty in finding and using scholarly information available at the institute and elsewhere.

title: Lincoln Corner Peshawar – IMSciences

Lincoln Corners are vibrant multi-media learning and engagement hubs established within Pakistan’s universities, public libraries, and cultural institutions. These corners offer young people avenues to discover American culture, history, and values. Currently, 19 Lincoln Corners operate nationwide, developed in collaboration with the United States Mission to Pakistan and leading local institutions.
Visitors to Lincoln Corner Peshawar can access a welcoming and resource-rich space to network, collaborate, and engage with reliable information about the United States. The diverse collection includes books, magazines, movies, and digital resources. These Corners serve as platforms for fostering cross-cultural understanding, developing skills, and facilitating meaningful conversations in a dynamic and inclusive setting. Notably, Lincoln Corner Peshawar is the first facility at IMSciences to be fully powered by solar energy, showcasing its commitment to sustainability and innovation.

title: Location – IMSciences
text:
IMSciences is situated in Phase VII Hayatabad: a newly developed zone of the modern planned suburb of Peshawar city. The town of Hayatabad is self-reliant as it provides for all the basic requirements of its residents: the town has its own network of hospitals, mosques, markets and supermarkets, primary, secondary and higher educational institutions, public transport, police and private security services, amusement parks, food chains, jogging tracks, sports grounds and gymnasia. The campus itself is picturesque for its beautiful landscapes surrounded by the scenic Tatara Mountains. Public transport, including local buses and cabs are regularly available on weekdays as well as on the weekends.

IMSciences Alumni Elections 2026
The IMSciences Alumni Association is pleased to announce the upcoming Alumni Elections 2026.
We encourage all alumni to stay informed and participate in this important process that will help shape the future of our alumni community.
IMSciences Alumni Registration Drive 2026
Calling all IMSciences Alumni!
The 2nd Elections of the IMSciences Alumni Association (IAA) are scheduled to be held in the last week of July 2026. To participate as a voter or candidate, you must be a registered member of the IMSciences Alumni Association.
Registration is FREE Open to all IMSciences Alumni No Membership Card required for voting
Last Date for Registration: July 10, 2026
Register now and become part of a growing network of IMSciences alumni while securing your eligibility to vote and contest in the Alumni Association Elections 2026.
Registration Link: https://imsalumni.imsciences.e du.pk/

while securing your eligibility to vote and contest in the Alumni Association Elections 2026.

Contest IAA Elections 2026
The IMSciences Alumni Association (IAA) invites eligible alumni to contest the IAA Elections 2026 and play a leadership role in strengthening the global IMSciences alumni community.
The elections will be held for both the National (Pakistan) Chapter and the following International Chapters:
United States | United Kingdom | Saudi Arabia | Europe | Canada | New Zealand | United Arab Emirates | Afghanistan | Australia
You can contest election for any one of the following positions.
â¢ President â¢ Vice President â¢ General Secretary â¢ Treasurer
Last Date to Submit Candidacy: July 15, 2026
Contest the Election: https://forms.gle/7EfXZxdTFJ8e 4Ny59
Only registered IMSciences alumni with no current working relationship with IMSciences are eligible to contest. A candidate can contest only one position and in only one chapter.

title: Office of Research Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC) – IMSciences
text:
Office of Research Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC)
Happening at ORIC
ORIC Team
Policies
Forms
Introduction
In today’s rapidly evolving world, the role of research, innovation, and commercialization in academic institutions has never been more crucial. At the Institute of Management Sciences (IMSciences), Peshawar, the Office of Research, Innovation, and Commercialization (ORIC) fulfills a vital need to cultivate a culture of academic inquiry and practical application to attain long term sustainability. ORIC at IMSciences was established in 2011 in consonance with the HEC Pakistan directives. It serves as a catalyst for advancing knowledge and addressing societal challenges through rigorous research endeavors. In alignment with the guidelines of the Higher Education Commission (HEC) of Pakistan, ORIC plays a pivotal role in promoting and supporting high-impact research initiatives across various disciplines.

title: Office of Research Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC) – IMSciences
text:
ation Commission (HEC) of Pakistan, ORIC plays a pivotal role in promoting and supporting high-impact research initiatives across various disciplines.
Our primary objective is to foster an environment where groundbreaking research is not only encouraged but also translated into tangible solutions that benefit both local communities and the broader society. By facilitating collaboration between academia, industry, and government sectors, ORIC at IMSciences strives to ensure that research outcomes have practical implications and contribute to national development goals. The ORIC, IMSciences aims to enhance the research capabilities of faculty members, researchers, and students by providing them with resources, funding opportunities, and strategic guidance. We emphasize ethical research practices and encourage innovative thinking that leads to entrepreneurial ventures. The ORIC IMSciences is committed to shape a future where research drives positive change and sustainable development.
Vision
To be a leading center for groundbreaking research, fostering innovation, and driving commercialization to create impactful solutions for society.
Mission

title: Office of Research Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC) – IMSciences
text:
e a leading center for groundbreaking research, fostering innovation, and driving commercialization to create impactful solutions for society.
Mission
Our mission is to promote pioneering research and foster innovation that leads to real-world solutions. We aim to bridge the gap between academic inquiry and practical application by supporting research that addresses societal challenges. By collaborating with academia, industry, and government sectors, we strive to ensure our research has a positive impact on local communities and society as a whole. We are committed to enhancing the research capabilities of our faculty, researchers, and students by providing resources, funding, and guidance. Through ethical practices and innovative thinking, we endeavor to drive positive change and support sustainable development.
Core Values
Excellence in Research: We are committed to conducting high-quality, impactful research that advances knowledge and addresses real-world challenges.
Innovation and Creativity: We encourage innovative thinking and creative problem-solving to develop new ideas and solutions that drive progress and development.

title: Office of Research Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC) – IMSciences
text:
and Creativity: We encourage innovative thinking and creative problem-solving to develop new ideas and solutions that drive progress and development.
Collaboration and Partnership: We believe in the power of collaboration, fostering partnerships with academia, industry, and government to enhance the reach and impact of our research.
Ethical Integrity: We uphold the highest standards of ethical conduct in all our research activities, ensuring honesty, transparency, and responsibility.
Community Engagement: We strive to make a positive impact on society by translating research into practical applications that benefit local communities and contribute to national development.
Scope of ORIC
The Office of Research, Innovation, and Commercialization (ORIC) at the Institute of Management Sciences (IMSciences), Peshawar, operates within a comprehensive framework designed to foster excellence in research, innovation, and commercialization. The scope of ORIC encompasses the following key areas:
Facilitate high-impact research across various disciplines by providing resources, funding opportunities, and strategic guidance to faculty, researchers, and students.

title: Office of Research Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC) – IMSciences
text:
mpact research across various disciplines by providing resources, funding opportunities, and strategic guidance to faculty, researchers, and students.
Encourage interdisciplinary research collaborations and initiatives that address societal challenges and contribute to national development goals.
Promote ethical research practices and ensure compliance with institutional, national, and international research standards.
Innovation and Entrepreneurship:
Foster a culture of innovation by supporting the development of new ideas, technologies, and solutions that have the potential to create significant social and economic impact.
Provide training, mentorship, and resources to aspiring entrepreneurs, enabling them to transform innovative ideas into viable business ventures.
Establish innovation hubs, incubators, and accelerators to support startups and facilitate the commercialization of research outcomes.
Industry-Academia Collaboration:
Strengthen partnerships between academia, industry, and government sectors to enhance the practical application of research findings.

title: Office of Research Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC) – IMSciences
text:
a Collaboration:
Strengthen partnerships between academia, industry, and government sectors to enhance the practical application of research findings.
Facilitate knowledge transfer and technology commercialization by bridging the gap between academic research and industry needs.
Organize industry-academia networking events, conferences, and workshops to foster collaboration and exchange of ideas.
Policy and Regulatory Compliance:
Ensure alignment with the guidelines and directives of the Higher Education Commission (HEC) of Pakistan, particularly in terms of research quality, innovation, and commercialization.
Implement policies and procedures that promote transparency, accountability, and ethical conduct in all research and innovation activities.
Regularly review and update ORIC policies to adapt to emerging trends and regulatory requirements.
Capacity Building and Training:
Enhance the research capabilities of faculty members, researchers, and students through workshops, seminars, and training programs on various aspects of research, innovation, and commercialization.

title: Office of Research Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC) – IMSciences
text:
mbers, researchers, and students through workshops, seminars, and training programs on various aspects of research, innovation, and commercialization.
Provide opportunities for professional development and skill enhancement in areas such as grant writing, project management, and intellectual property rights.
Foster a supportive and collaborative research environment that encourages continuous learning and improvement.
Community Engagement and Impact:
Translate research outcomes into practical solutions that benefit local communities and address pressing societal issues.
Engage with community stakeholders to identify research needs and priorities, ensuring that research initiatives are relevant and impactful.
Promote public awareness and understanding of the importance of research, innovation, and commercialization in driving social and economic progress.
By encompassing these areas, ORIC at IMSciences aims to create a dynamic and supportive ecosystem that promotes excellence in research, fosters innovation, and drives commercialization, ultimately contributing to the sustainable development and prosperity of society.

title: Psychological Wellness Center – IMSciences
text:
The Psychological Wellness Center
Welcome to the Psychological Wellness Center, your dedicated haven for mental and emotional well-being on the vibrant campus of IMSciences.
At our center, we understand that university life can bring a multitude of challenges, and we’re here to support both students and staff in navigating them. Our mission is to promote psychological wellness, foster resilience, and provide a safe space for individuals to explore and enhance their mental health.Through our professional counseling services, we aim to empower you on your journey towards a happier, healthier, and more fulfilling life.
Your mental wellness matters, and we at IMSciences are committed to facilitating you in thriving academically, emotionally, and personally.
Meet the Therapist
Maidah Waheed is a Clinical Psychologist and Psychotherapist.
She has an MS in Clinical Psychology from the National University of Sciences and Technology (NUST), and has a diverse experience working in the clinical, academic, research and development sector.

title: Psychological Wellness Center – IMSciences
text:
ational University of Sciences and Technology (NUST), and has a diverse experience working in the clinical, academic, research and development sector.
In clinical and counseling settings, she has worked with individuals, couples and families from varying backgrounds and phases of life. Her approach to psychotherapy is Person Centered, and adheres to the provision of ethical, evidence-based, culturally sensitive psychotherapeutic interventions that are tailored to the unique needs of the person(s). She is trained in psychological assessment and a variety of psychotherapeutic interventions, including but not limited to: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Eye- movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR), Marriage and Family Therapy (MFT), Trauma Focused Therapies, and Psychological First Aid, etc.
What can you reach out for?
Students and staff are welcome to reach out for mental health support, ranging from:
Mental health consultation and support
Psychoeducation and psychological first aid
Coping with distress and difficult emotions
Management of interpersonal and academic difficulties
Counseling and brief psychotherapy

Quality Enhancement is a unique and symbolic phrase in terms of Higher Education in Pakistan. Importance of Quality can be judged from the fact that rapid growth in higher education necessitates Quality Assurance in order to sustain current levels of academic standards and improve contemporary learning processes.
The Institute of Management Sciences, Peshawar (IMSciences) has committed itself to continuous improvement through quality management, assurance and enhancement as indicated in its strategic objectives below:-
Ensuring Relevance – Interact proactively with the world of work and community to cater emerging requirements.
Ensuring Quality of Teaching and Learning – Enhance existing provisions for sustained improvement in the quality of teaching and learning, and work progressively for implementation of best practices.
Strengthen Research – Further the Institute’s research capacity.

title: Research and Development Department – IMSciences
text: The Research and Development Department at the Institute of Management Sciences plays an integral role in guiding and overseeing the research efforts of students across all degree programs offered at the Institute. This department is the point of contact for proposal and thesis submissions and is the focal office for arranging proposal defenses. It is responsible for the meticulous processing of theses, internship reports, and Final Year Projects, from their initial submission to final approval. R&D Department works in close collaboration with the supervisors and Program Directors in the selection of qualified examiners, ensuring the rigorous evaluation of each thesis. Additionally, the department organizes and conducts comprehensive viva voce examinations and public defenses, providing a platform for students to present their research findings and defend their work before a panel of experts. As the directorate to the Board of Advance Studies and Research (BASR), the R&D Department serves as the central hub for fostering and promoting advanced research within the institute. It facilitates the development of innovative research projects, supports faculty and students in their resear

IMSciences Scholarships / Grants / Financial Aid
IMSciences has been continuously working alongside international development organizations, corporate sector and donor agencies to provide equal higher education opportunities to the marginalized communities of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa specifically and the country at large. The aim of strengthening Institute in resource generation is to facilitate and meet the increased expectations of financially constrained but talented current and prospective students of the Institute. The Institute encourages such students by providing real time financial aid opportunities in quality higher education and enables them with updated skills and knowledge to play their role in the regional and national development of our country.

title: Scholarships – IMSciences
text:
uality higher education and enables them with updated skills and knowledge to play their role in the regional and national development of our country.
IMSciences have maintained beneficial and transparent Corporate Foundation Relations (CFRs) with a significant number of stakeholders including Development Sector, Corporate Sector, IOs, NGOs, Govt of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Government of Pakistan, National and International Foundations and Partner Universities. These organizations include but not limited to USAID, Afghan/Pak Conflict Prevention Pool Political Section, British High Commission Department for International Development (DFID), Japanese International Cooperation Agency (JICA), AusAID, French Govt, USEFP, Singapore Govt, NMDs Secretariat, Khushhali Bank Limited (KBL), Bank of Khyber (Bok), Meezan Bank Limited (MBL), Faysal Bank Limited, Higher Education Commission of Pakistan (HEC), Multi Donor Post Disaster / Conflict Financial Aid, Chief Minister Education Endowment Fund (CMEEF), Govt of KPK, Govt. of Punjab, Pakistan Education Endowment Fund, Faysal Bank Limited as well as the Prime Minister Underdeveloped Areas and Ehsaas Scholarship Projects.

title: Scholarships – IMSciences
text:
of Punjab, Pakistan Education Endowment Fund, Faysal Bank Limited as well as the Prime Minister Underdeveloped Areas and Ehsaas Scholarship Projects.
Last year, IMSciences funded a significant number of students who had financial constraints and were unable to continue their education. In Financial Year 2024-25, approximately a total of 1,909 students which is 54.54 % of the total strength, has been provided financial support worth Rs. 242.134 Million, in 20 different Fully Funded Need and Merit Based Scholarship Programs, Merit Based Scholarships for Talented Students, Interest Free Student Loans, Siblings Fee Rebate, Work Study Programs and Global Cultural Exchange Programs.
Scholarships / Financial Aid Categories:
The Institute offers a comprehensive portfolio of financial aid / scholarships as per the following categories.
1. Fully Funded Need Based Merit Scholarship Programs:
These scholarship programs are mostly funded by the International Donor Organizations, Govt Sector, Corporate Foundations as well as Development Sector. These scholarships cover 100 % tuition fee support, accommodation, travelling, books and incidental charges for the whole duration of the study program.

title: Scholarships – IMSciences
text:
ese scholarships cover 100 % tuition fee support, accommodation, travelling, books and incidental charges for the whole duration of the study program.
2. Merit Based Scholarship Programs for Talented Students:
These scholarship programs are funded by IMSciences own resources and are awarded in all study programs to the top 10 % students of the total strength. They are provided with 65 to 75 % of tuition fee relaxation for the whole degree program.
There is another merit scholarship program which is semester wise and purely based on merit and these awards are granted to the most intelligent students of IMSciences who demonstrate their intellectual capabilities and achieve the maximum GPA in each Semester.
3. Fee Rebate Programs:
The Institute also offers attractive Fee Rebate Programs such as Sibling Fee Rebates, Selective Undergraduate and all MS Level Fee Rebate Programs etc.
4. Interest Free Student Loan Schemes:
The Institute has signed MoU with the Ihsan Trust Karachi (Meezan Bank Limited) and also students can avail interest free student loans to continue their studies at the Institute. Apart from this, students can also avail the National Bank Student Loan Scheme.

title: Scholarships – IMSciences
text:
nterest free student loans to continue their studies at the Institute. Apart from this, students can also avail the National Bank Student Loan Scheme.
5. International Cultural Exchange Programs:
There has been a significant increase in the number international cultural exchange programs and their beneficiary students at the Institute as follows.
Mevlana Student Exchange Program funded by Govt of Turkey
USEFP Global UGRAD Leadership Exchange Programme to USA
USEFP Global UGRAD Comparative Public Policy Exchange Programme to USA
USEFP Global UGRAD Pakistan North East South Asia Education Exchange Programme (NESA)
USEFP Global UGRAD Pakistan Student Exchange Women Leadership Programme
SUSI – Study of the United States Institutes for Student Leaders
French Govt Cultural Exchange Program
Mahvash and Jahangir Siddiqui Foundation and Sajjad Foundation (Singapore) sponsored Summer Program on Economic and Enterprise Development in National University of Singapore (NUS)
Global Internship Program at Timisoara (Romania)
AIESEC Internship in Columbia
AIESEC Nepal Global Volunteers Program
6. Work Study Programs:

6. Work Study Programs:
Under these programs a noticeable number of students are involved in on campus job opportunities during their student lives. They are not only associated as internees with different Functional Departments and Development Centers of the Institute but also they get financial benefit and exposure by contributing towards some well known Socio Economic and Development Projects of the Institute. One of the latest examples is IM | Durshal which is a collaboration between Institute of Management Sciences, Peshawar and KPITB (Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Information Technology Board). It is an incubation program for tech-entrepreneurs in KP, Pakistan.
7. Charity Begins at Home – Prof. Dr. Hidayatullah Need Based Merit Scholarship Program:
It was 31 st March 2011, when the Director IM|Sciences approved the faculty funded need based scholarship program to be named after the first Director of the Institute Prof. Dr. Hidayatullah (late), whose vision was to establish an exemplary management Institute at the doorstep to Central Asia.

title: Scholarships – IMSciences
text:
ector of the Institute Prof. Dr. Hidayatullah (late), whose vision was to establish an exemplary management Institute at the doorstep to Central Asia.
The purpose of this financial aid program was to consider all those needy students who were in the mid of their studies and were confronted with unforeseen circumstances like death of guardian, life threatening diseases, selling of family property or ornaments for medical expenses. These targeted needy students were always in search of prompt financial aid support and they often have missed the scholarships which are normally announced at the time of admission. As at that time they were not in such a need for financial assistance.
Charity begins at home stands right as all the faculty members generously contribute on monthly basis towards this valuable financial aid program, and till it’s commencement a total of 124 students have been provided tuition fee support for continuing their higher education, amounting Rs. 6,202,940/-. This initiative has been praised by the senior authorities of Higher Education Commission of Pakistan as well as different Vice Chancellors of Partner Universities.

IMSciences Fully Funded Scholarship for Students from Newly Merged Districts (NMDs) of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (Ex-FATA) for Undergraduate, MBA, and MS Level Programs

title: Scholarships and Financial Aid Programs – IMSciences
text:
Fully Funded Scholarship for Students from Newly Merged Districts (NMDs) of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (Ex-FATA) for Undergraduate, MBA, and MS Level Programs
Based on the development needs of the Newly Merged Districts (NMDs) region of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, IMSciences has achieved a significant milestone with the establishment of the IMSciences FATA Scholarship Program in cooperation with the FATA Secretariat, Peshawar. This program is designed to ensure equal access to education for students from the former FATA, offering fully funded scholarships for various academic levels at IMSciences. The program provides a total of twenty-five (25) scholarships: 13 for Undergraduate Programs, 8 for MBA, and 4 for MS Level Degree Programs. These scholarships cover full tuition fees and provide a monthly stipend for transportation, accommodation, books, and incidental expenses throughout the duration of the studies.

Chief Minister Education Endowment Fund (CMEEF) Merit and Need Based Scholarships for Undergraduate Level (BBA)
Under the supervision of the Higher Education Department (HED) of the Government of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP), the Institute of Management Sciences (IMSciences) has been selected as one of the ten implementing partner institutions for a special scholarship program. This opportunity is exclusively available for students enrolled in the BBA program at IMSciences. The scholarship will cover full tuition fees, accommodation, and a monthly stipend of Rs. 5,000 to support books, travel, and other incidental expenses throughout the duration of the program. Awarding of the scholarship is subject to final approval from the HED – Government of KP.

HEC Need Based Merit Scholarship Program in all Undergraduate Level Programs (4 Years Degree Programs)
Under the supervision of the Higher Education Commission (HEC), this Fully Funded Need-Based Merit Scholarship Program is designed to support study programs with the highest marketability in Pakistan’s corporate sector. It aims to provide equal opportunities to talented students from marginalized areas who are unable to pursue higher education due to financial constraints. A total of twenty-five (25) fully funded scholarships are available under this scheme in all twelve (12) undergraduate level study programs. The scholarship covers full tuition fees and provides a monthly stipend for accommodation, books, travel, and incidental expenses for the entire duration of study at IMSciences. These scholarships are subject to HEC approval and the availability of funds.

Government of Punjab has launched a major initiative for award of 5000 scholarships to the enrolled students in 68 different disciplines at Public Sector Universities and HED Colleges in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa to provide financial assistance to academically talented and deserving students, fostering leadership and community service among the youth. The program seeks to remove financial barriers for students from diverse backgrounds enabling them to pursue higher education and achieve their career aspirations. IMSciences has been awarded a total of fifty (50) scholarship awards last year. These scholarships are subject to the approval of the respective donor, Govt of Punjab. The official web portal for student’s registration including relevant details and eligibility criteria is already activated i.e. https://honhaarscholarship.punjabhec.gov.pk

Provision of Higher Education Opportunities for the Students of Balochistan & Erstwhile FATA
Under the supervision of Higher Education Commission of Pakistan this beneficent scholarship program is available in undergraduate level all study programs. These scholarships are merit based and targeted towards outstanding candidates having Domicile / Local Certificate of Balochistan and erstwhile FATA. This scholarship award offers up to Rs. 60,000/- tuition fee and Rs. 41,000/- stipend per semester and renewable upon satisfactory academic performance. All details regarding advertisement, eligibility criteria, selection procedure and how to apply can be found on HEC website at https://www.hec.gov.pk/english/scholarshipsgrants/BF-III/Pages/default.aspx

HEC Undergraduate Scholarship Program for the Students of Gilgit – Baltistan in Top Pakistani Universities / Institutions
The main aim of the program is to provide scholarships to the students from GB to equip the youth with the latest education for upcoming challenges. Gilgit Baltistan is a critically important area and is the terminal point of CPEC road whereas the Gawadar is on the other end. The program will provide financial assistance to the students to pursue their higher studies in all the top Public Sector universities / institutions of Pakistan for the development of their area and the country.

Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Education Foundation Scholarship Program for Undergraduate Level (04 Years) and Graduate Level (02 Years Masters/MS)
In cooperation with the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Education Foundation, Government of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, these scholarships are awarded based on merit and affordability. The grant provides Rs. 150,000 per annum for tuition fee support. The scholarship is renewable for the second year contingent upon satisfactory academic performance. 8 seats in total are available: 4 for undergraduate studies and 4 for MS and MBA programs. Only applicants domiciled in KP (including Newly Merged Districts) are eligible to apply. 50% of the scholarship seats are reserved for female students. These scholarships are subject to approval from KPEF – Government of KP.

Pakistan Education Endowment Fund (PEEF) Scholarship Program for Students enrolled in BS Artificial Intelligence
In collaboration with the Pakistan Education Endowment Fund (PEEF), Government of Pakistan, these scholarships are awarded to students enrolled in the BS Artificial Intelligence program—a four-year undergraduate degree with high marketability and demand. A total of 7 seats are available under this scholarship scheme. Each scholarship includes Rs. 60,000/- tuition fee support and a Rs. 15,000/- stipend per semester. Disbursement of each scholarship installment is contingent upon satisfactory academic performance, as per the applicable rules. These scholarships are subject to approval from PEEF – Government of Pakistan.

Albert Einstein German Academic Refugee Initiative Fund (DAFI) for Afghan Refugee Students in all Undergraduate Programs (4 Years Degree Programs)
Inspire Pakistan is implementing Albert Einstein German Academic Refugee Initiative Fund (DAFI) in partnership with UNHCR since 2014 throughout Pakistan, under which Refugee students studying in tertiary institutions in Pakistan receive scholarships. The scholarship amount covers their tuition fee, books and travelling allowance. DAFI scholarships are awarded for a maximum of four years of study at bachelor or equivalent degree levels in IMSciences, Peshawar. Eligible students will have to apply to Inspire Pakistan through their prescribed application form after their call for applications.

HEC - DAAD EFFAL Scholarship For Afghan Refugees in Pakistan in all Two (2) Years Graduate Level Degree Programs
The DAAD fully funded Master’s scholarships are available for male and female Afghan refugees residing in Pakistan. Offered through the Higher Education Commission (HEC) Pakistan and funded by the Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development, Germany, this programme enables students to pursue two-year Master’s degrees at Institute of Management Sciences across all disciplines. The initiative aims to promote academic and scientific advancement by supporting individuals aspiring to become future academic and professional leaders at regional and global levels. Benefits include full tuition fees, a monthly stipend, housing allowance, and book allowance as determined by the Higher Education Commission and Govt of Pakistan.

Punjab Education Endowment Fund – Scholarships for Other Provinces
The Punjab Educational Endowment Fund (PEEF) has allocated special scholarship quotas for talented and needy students of other provinces, AJK, Gilgit Baltistan and Islamabad Capital Territory. These scholarships are awarded to the pass outs of public sector institutions to pursue Intermediate, Graduation and Master’s level education in their respective province or any other educational institution of Pakistan.
Pakistan Bait-ul-Mal Need Based Merit Scholarships
Pakistan Bait-ul-Mal (PBM) is providing Financial Assistance to poor deserving students having good academic track record and studying on regular basis in Government Universities / Institutes. Students with monthly family income of Rs. 15,000/- and are not dependent on Govt Servant are eligible for this program. PBM provides to Rs. 100,000/- per annum per family as education assistance, subject to fulfillment of eligibility criteria. Eligible students will have to apply in the District Zakat Office of the Pakistan Bait-ul-Mal.

IMSciences Merit Based Partial Scholarships
The Institute offers a partial scholarship to 10% of students admitted to the following programs: BBA, BS Business & Analytics, BCS, BS Software Engineering, BS Social Sciences, BS Cyber Security, BS Artificial Intelligence, BS Data Science, BS Economics, BS (A&F), BS English, BS Psychology, and MBA (2 years). For the MBA (2 years) program, 5% of these scholarship seats are reserved for the institute’s own students/graduates, with the remaining 5% awarded to other students. Scholarship recipients receive a fee reduction ranging from 65% to 75%. The scholarships are renewable for the duration of the program. The Admission Office will shortlist students based on the Aggregate Merit List and notify successful candidates accordingly.

This meritorious award is amounting Rs. 10,000/- per semester. These scholarships are granted in all semesters at undergraduate and Graduate level programs.
IMSciences Alumni 50 % Tuition Fee Waiver in all MS/MBA and PhD Level Programs
Based on the directives of the Director IMSciences Peshawar, all those applicants who are also alumni of the Institute, will be eligible for fifty (50) percent tuition fee waiver in all eight (8) MS level programs i.e. MS Management, MS Project Management, MS Computer Science, MS Data Science, MS Economics, MS Islamic Banking & Finance, MS Development Studies and MS Governance & Public Policy.
IMSciences Inflationary Rebate / Waiver of 25% Tuition Fee in all MS Level Programs

title: Scholarships and Financial Aid Programs – IMSciences
text:
Finance, MS Development Studies and MS Governance & Public Policy.
IMSciences Inflationary Rebate / Waiver of 25% Tuition Fee in all MS Level Programs
Based on the directives of the Director IMSciences Peshawar, keeping in view the high inflation in the economy, all applicants will be eligible for twenty five (25 %) percent tuition fee waiver in all all eight (8) MS level programs i.e. MS Management, MS Project Management, MS Computer Science, MS Data Science, MS Economics, MS Islamic Banking & Finance, MS Development Studies and MS Governance & Public Policy as well as PhD level degree programs.
IMSciences Tuition Fee Waiver in all MS Level Programs

title: Scholarships and Financial Aid Programs – IMSciences
text:
MS Development Studies and MS Governance & Public Policy as well as PhD level degree programs.
IMSciences Tuition Fee Waiver in all MS Level Programs
IMSciences Peshawar introduced a new merit based partial tuition fee waiver especially for students of MS Level Degree Programs. These scholarship awards are available in all MS Level Degree Programs i.e. MS Management, MS Project Management, MS Computer Science, MS Data Science, MS Economics, MS Islamic Banking & Finance, MS Development Studies and MS Governance & Public Policy. Twenty (20) % Fee Waiver will be provided to top 40 % students in all three semesters. These meritorious awards are with subject to approval from the competent authority.
Dr. Hidayatullah Need Based Merit Scholarships

title: Scholarships and Financial Aid Programs – IMSciences
text:
ll three semesters. These meritorious awards are with subject to approval from the competent authority.
Dr. Hidayatullah Need Based Merit Scholarships
This scholarship program is established and funded by the faculty and staff members of IMSciences for the benefit of those financially needy students who face financial hindrances in the middle or final stages of their study program. Under this financial aid program, IMSciences has successfully facilitated a total of One Hundred and Thirty Five (135) such students from different disciplines and backgrounds for the completion of their higher education studies in IMSciences. More than 8.35 Million Rupees have been distributed among selected students.
IMSciences Sibling / Brother-Sister 50 % Tuition Fee Rebate
Through this program, IMSciences offers 50% fee rebate to students if their brother/sister study at IMSciences. Students have to apply each semester to avail this fee rebate.
IMSciences Fully Funded Scholarships for Students with Disability

title: Scholarships and Financial Aid Programs – IMSciences
text:
study at IMSciences. Students have to apply each semester to avail this fee rebate.
IMSciences Fully Funded Scholarships for Students with Disability
As per the instructions of Higher Education Commission of Pakistan, IMSciences has formed Institutional Accessibility Committee to assess the financial and other academic needs of students with Disabilities. IMSciences provides a total of five (5) scholarships every year at Undergraduate, Graduate and Ph. D Levels. The scholarship award covers 100 % Tuition Fee, along with additional financial assistance for any other necessary expenses such as living expenses, books charges, travelling cost or any other expense as determined by the committee.
Mevlana Exchange Program, Istanbul University – Republic of Turkey

title: Scholarships and Financial Aid Programs – IMSciences
text:
books charges, travelling cost or any other expense as determined by the committee.
Mevlana Exchange Program, Istanbul University – Republic of Turkey
Mevlana Exchange Program is a program which aims the exchange of students and academic staff between the Turkish higher education institutions and higher education institutions of other countries. This program is funded by Republic of Turkey. Different from other exchange programs, Mevlana Exchange Program includes all higher education institutions in the world regardless of their region. For updates, please visit http://mevlana.istanbul.edu.tr/en.
Fauji Foundation Excellence Award
Fauji Foundation Educational Stipend Scheme is the oldest education welfare measure of the Fauji Foundation for the welfare of Ex-servicemen and their families. The benefits of this scheme are reaching Beneficiaries studying in schools, colleges, universities as well as those undergoing professional or technical education. It was introduced in 1954 and has continued ever-since, uninterrupted.Â Â Â For further details please visit http://www.fauji.org.pk/fauji/welfare/stipends
Shaheen Excellence Award and Fellowship Scheme

title: Scholarships and Financial Aid Programs – IMSciences
text:
ce, uninterrupted.Â Â Â For further details please visit http://www.fauji.org.pk/fauji/welfare/stipends
Shaheen Excellence Award and Fellowship Scheme
Shaheen Excellence Award of Rs. 50,000/- (one time) will be awarded to Sons / Daughters of Ex / Servicing PAF Personnel securing first position in final examination from any HSSC Board or HEC recognized universities in Pakistan in all recognized disciplines. While Shaheen Fellowship Scheme is for higher education in any disciplines at Bachelor level & Master level studies. The fellowship scheme started in year 1997. There are a total of 150 fellowships available every year on merit to Bachelor level students and 50 to Master level students. For further details, please visit http://www.shaheenfoundation.com/scholarship.php
Ihsan Trust Financial Assistance Program for Higher Education

title: Scholarships and Financial Aid Programs – IMSciences
text:
ents. For further details, please visit http://www.shaheenfoundation.com/scholarship.php
Ihsan Trust Financial Assistance Program for Higher Education
One of the major focuses of the trust is in the domain of providing Qarz-e-Hasna (Interest free loan) for Education to the needy and deserving students of major and leading universities of Pakistan. Through this scheme the Ihsan Trust bears the educational expenses of the students on purely “Need cum Merit” basis in order to enable them to complete their Higher Education. The amount of Qarz-e-Hasna sanctioned to the selected student depends on his/her need and eligibility. During the study period, the student has to pay a nominal, token repayment amount (depending upon the financial position of the candidates, his/her parents/guardian’s monthly income) to the Ihsan Trust as repayment. After the completion of his/her education, the repayment amount for the Qarz-e-Hasna is to be adjusted according to the income of the student. For further details, please visit https://www.ihsantrust.org/education.html

title: IMSciences 20Admission 20Policy 2026
text:
1
i.
Admissions shall be open to all candidates strictly on the basis of merit, subject to fulfillment of
prescribed criteria, without discrimination on the grounds of race, gender, ethnicity or socio-
economic status.
ii.
Annual admissions to all degree programs shall be conducted either for the Fall Semester or the
Spring Semester.
iii.
The number of students admitted to each program shall be determined by the Admission
Committee at the time of admission, on a case-by-case basis.
iv.
All information submitted by applicants must be supported by attested copies of relevant
documents, including transcripts and certificates. Any document lacking official verification shall
be deemed invalid.
v.
Applicants are solely responsible for providing complete, accurate and timely information to the
Admission Office before closing date. Incomplete or incorrect submissions shall not be considered.
vi.
Submission of false, forged or fraudulent documents or misrepresentation in the admission form,
shall be dealt with in accordance with prevailing laws. In such cases, the applicant’s admission
request shall not be processed and any admission granted shall be cancelled.
vii.

title: IMSciences 20Admission 20Policy 2026
text:
dance with prevailing laws. In such cases, the applicant’s admission
request shall not be processed and any admission granted shall be cancelled.
vii.
The Institute reserves the right to revoke the degree of any candidate, at any stage, who secured
admission through falsified documents or misrepresentation.
viii.
Partial scholarships shall be awarded to 10% of students admitted to the following programs: BBA,
BS Accounting & Finance, BS Business Analytics, BS Computer Science (General), BSCS
Software Engineering, BSCS Data Science, BSCS Artificial Intelligence, BSCS Cyber Security,
BS Social Sciences, BS Economics, BS English, BS Psychology, MBA. For the MBA program,
5% of the scholarships shall be reserved for the Institute’s own graduate applicants, while the
remaining 5% shall be awarded to other students.
ix.
The Admission Committee reserves the right to increase, decrease or abolish the specified
percentage of scholarships on a case-by-case basis.
x.
There shall be no reserved quota seats for admission to any program, except for a 2% quota for
individuals with disabilities (with mandatory physical accessibility) and a 2% quota for minority

title: IMSciences 20Admission 20Policy 2026
text:
admission to any program, except for a 2% quota for
individuals with disabilities (with mandatory physical accessibility) and a 2% quota for minority
candidates across all academic programs.
xi.
Detailed eligibility requirements for admission to various programs of the Institute are enumerated
below.

title: Student 20Handbook
text:
Student Handbook 2023
Onwards
Institute of Management Sciences Peshawar
1
ABOUT THE STUDENT HANDBOOK
The Institute of Management Sciences (IMSciences) is a premier public sector business school of the
country. The Institute was established in May 1995 as a constituent institute of Peshawar University. Due
to significant achievements in a short span of time, the Government of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa decided in
1999 to develop it into a model institution of the country. It was given an autonomous status vide
Ordinance No. XXXVII of 2002 and was placed under the Board of Governors (BOG) headed by the
Governor Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. The Institute is dedicated to its unique approach in the region of providing
management education based in cutting-edge research and comprehensive training.
The main purpose of the ‘Student Handbook’ is to introduce you to the facilities, procedures and codes of
conduct relating to students pursuing education at the Institute of Management Sciences (IMSciences),
Hayatabad, Peshawar. It will sufficiently guide you as to how can you appropriately utilize the
opportunities available at IMSciences. The instructions/information contained herein are subject to change

title: Student 20Handbook
text:
as to how can you appropriately utilize the
opportunities available at IMSciences. The instructions/information contained herein are subject to change
from time to time as and when deemed necessary and appropriate on the part of IMSciences in order to
meet the demands of the day. The procedures and instructions highlighted in the Student Handbook are
applicable to all students of IMSciences unless otherwise specifically mentioned to the contrary provided
that the Student Handbook is not a substitute for the comprehensive documents notified from time to time
regarding Admissions, Academic Policies, Programs of Study, Grading and Evaluation, etc; etc.
The students are, therefore, advised in their own interest to regularly consult the notifications, circulars,
announcements issued from time to time, in addition to this Handbook. No relaxation would be allowed on
the pretext of ‘being ignorant’.

CHAPTER 1
SEMESTER RULES
1. SHORT TITLE COMMENCEMENT AND APPLICATION
i.
These rules shall be called the Institute of Management Sciences, Peshawar Semester Rules-
2017.
ii.
These rules shall come into force with immediate effect.
iii.
These rules shall be applicable to all degree programs offered by Institute of Management
Sciences, Peshawar under semester system.
2. DEFINITION
i.
“Institute” means Institute of Management Sciences, Peshawar.
ii.
“Director” means Director of the Institute.
iii.
“Joint Director” means Joint Director of the Institute.
iv.
“Program Coordinator” means a faculty member of the Institute who has been assigned the
duty of coordinating academic activities of that degree program, by the Director of the
Institute.
v.
“Academic Program” means a program of studies which leads to the award of degree to the
students in that program upon successful completion of all its requirements.
vi.
“Course” means a regular / full time course of study leading to the partial / successful
completion of the program, as the case may be.
vii.

title: Student 20Handbook
text:
ents.
vi.
“Course” means a regular / full time course of study leading to the partial / successful
completion of the program, as the case may be.
vii.
“Grade Point Average (GPA)” means the semester grade points earned in a semester or in the
course of study divided by total number of credit hours.
viii.
Cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA) is the summation of semester grade points (GPAs)
of all semesters including thesis divided by the total number of credit hours taken by a student.
ix.
Admission Committee means a committee appointed for the purposes of interviewing,
examining and selecting candidates for admission who qualify the prescribed eligibility criteria
for admission.
x.
“Unfair Means Committee” means a committee constituted under these rules
xi.
“Semester Committee” means a committee constituted under these rules.
4
3. ACADEMIC YEAR / SESSION
The academic year / session shall comprise of two regular semesters and an optional summer
semester.
i.
Fall semester shall normally start from August/September and will last till January.
ii.
Spring semester shall normally start from January/February and last till May/ June.
iii.

title: Student 20Handbook
text:
y start from August/September and will last till January.
ii.
Spring semester shall normally start from January/February and last till May/ June.
iii.
A summer semester of 8 weeks duration may be offered during summer vacations i-e June
to August, in special circumstances. The contact hours in summer semester may be doubled to
ensure that the course is completely taught during the summer session.
4. DURATION OF SEMESTER
i.
Each semester shall be of 18 weeks duration; out of which 16 weeks shall be reserved for
teaching, and 2 weeks for examinations.
ii.
There shall be a “teaching break” to be called “Semester Break” of 1 week after every
semester; this break shall be used for preparation/ submission of result and admission /
registration processes of next semester.
iii.
There shall be two examinations in one semester i-e Mid Term (8 weeks teaching +9
th week
for examination) and Final Term (8 weeks of teaching +18th week of examination). iv. There
shall be 100% lecture delivery for considering a semester as a valid semester.
iv.
However, if under some exigency the teaching in the Institute is suspended, the requirement

title: Student 20Handbook
text:
livery for considering a semester as a valid semester.
iv.
However, if under some exigency the teaching in the Institute is suspended, the requirement
of 100% could be brought to 14 teaching weeks upon the recommendation of Semester
Committee, which shall be approved by the Director of the Institute.
5. ACADEMIC CALENDAR
The Institute will publish a schedule of complete academic year, including fall and spring
semester for the convenience of the students, staff and faculty members. The calendar will
include the following information.
 Semester starting date
 Holidays during the semester
 Semester ending date
 Mid Term / Final Term Exam
 Result notification date
5
6. CREDIT HOURS
i.
A credit hour means teaching a theory course for 60 minutes each week throughout the
semester.
ii.
One credit hour in Computer Lab or practical work/project would require lab contact of three
hours per week throughout the semester.
iii.
The credit hours are denoted by two digits within brackets with a hyphen in between. The first
digit represents the theory part while the second (right side) digits represent the practical.

title: Student 20Handbook
text:
its within brackets with a hyphen in between. The first
digit represents the theory part while the second (right side) digits represent the practical.
a. Thus 3 means three credit hours of theory, while 4 (3-1) means a total of four credit,
of which three are of theory while one credit hour is for Lab/Practical and 3 (2-1)
means a total of 3 credit, of which two are of theory while one credit hour is for
Lab/Practical.
b. The weekly contact hours of a three credit hours course will be three, the contact
hours of a 4(3-1) course will be six.
7. TEACHING METHODOLOGY
i.
Teaching at the Institute shall be conducted through lectures, discussions, case studies and
practical work in laboratories, demonstrations, field work, seminars, tutorials, audio- visual aids,
video conferencing, and study tours.
ii.
Teaching shall be conducted by the faculty member of the Institute or such other persons as
may be declared to be a faculty member by the competent authority of the Institute.
iii.
Medium of instruction for teaching and examination will be English.
8. COURSE FILE
Maintenance of Course File is mandatory for all faculty members teaching a course(s) at the Institute.

The Course File shall have complete record of all relevant academic activities undertaken during the

each in the checklists given below.
